description_long: This object is a rectangular stone stele featuring intricate carvings and hieroglyphic inscriptions typical of ancient Egyptian monuments. The front face is adorned with detailed reliefs of human figures in profile, flanking a central vertical panel, and surrounded by dense columns of hieroglyphs. The surface is weathered but the carvings remain prominent, with the figures and symbols deeply incised into the stone. The sides of the object are very thin, indicating it is a slab rather than a freestanding pillar. The color is a uniform pale beige, resembling limestone or sandstone, and the overall form is flat and upright. The top edge is straight, and the base appears slightly irregular, possibly due to age or damage.
description: A flat, rectangular stone stele with detailed Egyptian-style carvings and hieroglyphic inscriptions, featuring human figures and symbols on a pale, weathered surface.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full extent of the hieroglyphic inscriptions and relief carvings, with two human figures and a central vertical panel clearly visible.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the extreme thinness of the object, with minimal visible detail except the slab's edge.
description_view_2: The rear view is similar to the front, displaying the same carvings and inscriptions, though the reliefs may appear slightly less pronounced due to lighting.
description_view_3: The right side view, like the left, highlights the object's thin profile and shows little surface detail.
attribute_inscription: Dense hieroglyphic inscriptions cover the upper and side portions of the front face, carved in vertical columns. The script is ancient Egyptian, deeply incised, and mostly legible.
attribute_ornamentation: The front face features low-relief carvings of human figures in profile, as well as decorative hieroglyphic motifs. The ornamentation is concentrated on the front and is stylistically Egyptian.
attribute_base_design: The base is flat and slightly irregular, with no visible steps or platforms. It appears to be a simple extension of the main slab, with some weathering at the bottom edge.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and straight, with no pointed or pyramidal features. There are no unique decorations or embellishments at the top.
